notes: Umbrella brand for DEC's integrated TP hardware and software product suite; announced 1988; includes ACMS, DECintact, DECdtm, DECdta, Rdb/VMS, DECforms, VAXft; Kastner led marketing work for this announcement

Defines modularization and distribution structure common to DECtp products; six components: application program, resource manager, transaction manager, communication manager, presentation manager, request manager; client/server model

Embedded in VMS OS kernel; implements optimized two-phase commit protocol; supports distributed atomic transactions across multiple resource managers and nodes; VAXcluster-optimized blocking reduction

tech_name: VAX ACMS (Application Control and Management System)
notes: First introduced 1984; TP monitor for large complex TP applications; 4GL task definition language; deferred task model of queuing; layered on VMS; implements DECdta resource manager and request manager functions

Originally third-party product; purchased and introduced by DEC 1988; TP monitor using 3GL API library; message-based queuing model; installed in major financial institutions and manufacturing sites; includes own presentation manager and transaction manager
Relational database for VMS; implements SQL standard; uses KODA kernel; supports snapshot transactions for online backup; group commit optimization; survives as Oracle Rdb
CODASYL network database product for VMS; uses KODA kernel; on-line schema changes via DRU (Database Restructuring Utility); provides CODASYL data manipulation language

Physical subsystem shared by Rdb/VMS and VAX DBMS; handles all I/O, buffer management, concurrency control, transaction consistency, locking, journaling, access methods; provides group commit processing
Operating system for VAX; base layer for all DECtp products; Version 5.4 used in TPC Benchmark A; includes SMP support, Distributed Lock Manager, condition handling facility; survives as OpenVMS
Forms management product; first implementation of ANSI/ISO FIMS standard (CODASYL FIMS); provides device-independent human interface; implements DECdta presentation manager component

First fault-tolerant VAX system; dual-zone architecture with duplicate hardware; four-phase verification strategy; uses FTSS software for error recovery; designed for continuous operation TP environments
Layered software product for VAXft 3000; provides software error recovery for failed I/O devices; manages fault thresholds and device reconfiguration
DEC's mainframe-class VAX; achieved 69.4 tpsA-Local on audited TPC Benchmark A with 256 MB memory and 16 RA92 disk drives; CPU utilization over 90 percent at MQTh
Midrange VAX using same CMOS chip as VAX 6000 Model 400 series; achieved 21.6 tpsA-Local on audited TPC Benchmark A with 64 MB memory and 18 RF31 disk drives
4-processor VAX; used for KODA group commit algorithm experiments; each processor rated 36 VUP; 14.4 VUPS total; bottlenecked at 300-500 TPS depending on commit design
Tightly coupled multiprocessor family; Model 440 referenced in analytical model as dynamic parameter example; XMI bus; used in TP benchmark validation studies
Closely coupled distributed system; provides node failover for database availability; DECdtm optimized for VAXcluster to reduce two-phase commit blocking; referenced in multiple papers
DEC's networking architecture (DNA); Phase IV used in TPC Benchmark A test configurations; DECnet-VMS used for front-end to back-end communication

Industry-standard TP benchmark simulating banking environment; standardized November 1989; metrics: tpsA-Local and tpsA-Wide; succeeded by TPC-B and TPC-C; DEC participated in 1989 standardization activity
X/Open Transaction Processing Working Group developing standard application programming interfaces; DECdta architecture designed to align with these standards; basis for later XA standard
ISO wire protocol for multi-vendor TP system interoperability; cited as future DECdta communication manager interoperability target
IBM Systems Network Architecture Logical Unit 6.2; explicitly named as example communication manager protocol in DECdta architecture
Standard query language implemented by VAX Rdb/VMS; cited as resource manager interface standard in DECdta

Atomic commit protocol ensuring all-or-nothing transaction semantics across distributed resource managers; central protocol in DECdtm; DEC implemented optimized variant with VAXcluster-specific blocking reduction
DEC's network architecture; referenced as companion architecture to DECdta; DECtp products designed to integrate with DNA
DEC's proprietary predecessor to TPC Benchmark A; used to validate analytical model; results published CMG 1989; model validated against multiple VAX configurations running DebitCredit
DEC record management services; mentioned as COBOL file operations resource manager interface; part of VMS; supports sequential and random file access
DEC terminal server protocol; used in TPC Benchmark A front-end configuration for RTE terminal emulation via DECserver
